category.label: Backtesting
category.description: Building a backtest you can actually trust. In-sample / out-of-sample discipline, walk-forward validation, multiple-testing correction, and the pitfalls that turn ML pipelines into expensive overfitting.

entries.title: Backtesting Discipline (IS / OOS, Costs, Look-Ahead)
entries.subtitle: Run a backtest you can trust: in-sample / out-of-sample splits, walk-forward, realistic costs, point-in-time data, look-ahead avoidance.

entries.title: Walk-Forward Validation
entries.subtitle: Why rolling windows, anchored vs sliding, and stitched OOS curves are how you avoid mistaking parameter luck for skill.

entries.title: Multiple-Testing Correction (DSR, PBO, Bonferroni, FDR)
entries.subtitle: When you try 200 strategies, some will look great by luck alone. Deflated Sharpe Ratio, Probability of Backtest Overfitting, Bonferroni, and FDR — and which to use when.

entries.subtitle: Where machine-learning research goes wrong: leakage via cross-validation, label engineering, hyperparameter overfitting, and feature stability under regime shifts.
